Add-on Treatment.evidence: Abiraterone acetate plus prednisolone for metastatic patients starting hormone therapy: 5-year follow-up results from the STAMPEDE randomised trial (NCT00268476)
Add-on Treatment.plan_extraction_plan: The add-on treatment is "Abiraterone acetate plus prednisolone". This is explicitly stated in the title and abstract, and further detailed in the "Procedures" section.

Adverse Events - N (%) | All-Cause Grade.evidence: Toxicity at 4 years postrandomisation was similar, with 16% patients in each group reporting grade 3 or higher toxicity.
Adverse Events - N (%) | All-Cause Grade.plan_extraction_plan: Locate the sentence in Section 2.4 "Outcome measures and statistical analysis" that states: "Toxicity at 4 years postrandomisation was similar, with 16% patients in each group reporting grade 3 or higher toxicity." The text reports "16% patients in each group reporting grade 3 or higher toxicity" at 4 years postrandomisation. It does not explicitly distinguish between "all-cause" and "treatment-related" adverse events. The count (N) is not provided.

Adverse Events - N (%) | All-Cause Grade.evidence: Toxicity at 4 years postrandomisation was similar, with 16% patients in each group reporting grade 3 or higher toxicity.

The text reports "16% patients in each group reporting grade 3 or higher toxicity" at 4 years postrandomisation. Adverse Events - N (%) | Treatment-relat.evidence: Toxicity at 4 years postrandomisation was similar, with 16% patients in each group reporting grade 3 or higher toxicity.
Adverse Events - N (%) | Treatment-relat.plan_extraction_plan: Locate the sentence in Section 2.4 "Outcome measures and statistical analysis" that states: "Toxicity at 4 years postrandomisation was similar, with 16% patients in each group reporting grade 3 or higher toxicity." The text reports "16% patients in each group reporting grade 3 or higher toxicity" at 4 years postrandomisation. While "toxicity" in a clinical trial context often implies treatment-related adverse events, it is not explicitly stated as "treatment-related". The count (N) is not provided.

In Section 4 "DISCUSSION", locate the sentence: "...and no grade 4 or 5 toxicity." This statement refers to the "worst reported grade of toxicity" for patients in the SOC + AAP group (treatment arm) at 2 years after randomisation. The text explicitly states "no grade 4 or 5 toxicity" for a subset of patients at 2 years. While not explicitly stated as "treatment-related", "toxicity" in this context is generally understood to refer to adverse effects of the treatment.

In Section 4 "DISCUSSION", locate the sentence: "...and no grade 4 or 5 toxicity." This statement refers to the "worst reported grade of toxicity" for patients in the SOC-alone group (control arm) at 2 years after randomisation. The text explicitly states "no grade 4 or 5 toxicity" for a subset of patients at 2 years. While not explicitly stated as "treatment-related", "toxicity" in this context is generally understood to refer to adverse effects of the treatment.

COE_RCT_IND_OVERALL_RJ.plan_extraction_plan: The PDF describes the trial design and execution, including aspects relevant to risk of bias (e.g., "There was no blinding to treatment allocation for practical reasons"), but it does not provide an explicit "overall risk judgment of individual criteria of evidence" using a structured framework (e.g., low/high/unclear risk of bias for specific domains).

Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.chunk_id: Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1::1
Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.group_name: Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1
Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.plan_extraction_plan: The first experimental arm (SOC + AAP) includes Abiraterone acetate and prednisolone. Abiraterone acetate is an androgen biosynthesis inhibitor. Prednisolone is a corticosteroid. This information is found in the text.

Control Arm.evidence: The comparison randomised patients 1:1 to SOC-alone with or without daily abiraterone acetate 1000 mg + prednisolone 5 mg (SOC + AAP), continued until disease progression.
Control Arm.plan_extraction_plan: The control arm is referred to as "SOC-alone" or "SOC" (Standard-of-Care alone). This is explicitly stated in the abstract and methods section. From the abstract (Chunk 5, page 3, "CANCER THERAPY AND PREVENTION" section): "The comparison randomised patients 1:1 to SOC-alone with or without daily abiraterone acetate 1000 mg + prednisolone 5 mg (SOC + AAP), continued until disease progression." From the "Randomisation and masking" section (Chunk 6, page 4): "Allocation was 1:1 to standard-of-care (SOC-alone) only group or SOC-alone with abiraterone acetate and prednisolone/prednisone group (SOC + AAP)." The tables (Table 1 on page 5, Table 2 on page 6, Table 3 on page 8) and figures (Figure 2 on page 7, Figure 3 on page 9) also use "SOC-alone" or "SOC" to denote the control group.

Control Arm - N.plan_extraction_plan: The total number of participants in the control arm (SOC-alone) is reported as 502. This can be found in: Figure 1 (CONSORT diagram). Locate the box "Allocated to SOC" and extract the value "N = 502".

Docetaxel administration - N (%) | Treat.evidence: Reported use of docetaxel within 1 year after first progression was higher among patients allocated to SOC + AAP (32% SOC-alone and 40% SOC + AAP, Chi-square P = .048).
Docetaxel administration - N (%) | Treat.plan_extraction_plan: On page 8, in the "DISCUSSION" section, the text states: "Reported use of docetaxel within 1 year after first progression was higher among patients allocated to SOC + AAP (32% SOC-alone and 40% SOC + AAP, Chi-square P = .048)." The treatment arm is "SOC + AAP".

Docetaxel administration - N (%) | Contr.evidence: Reported use of docetaxel within 1 year after first progression was higher among patients allocated to SOC + AAP (32% SOC-alone and 40% SOC + AAP, Chi-square P = .048).
Docetaxel administration - N (%) | Contr.plan_extraction_plan: On page 8, in the "DISCUSSION" section, the text states: "Reported use of docetaxel within 1 year after first progression was higher among patients allocated to SOC + AAP (32% SOC-alone and 40% SOC + AAP, Chi-square P = .048)." The control arm is "SOC-alone".

Gleason score - N (%) | \u2264 7 | Treat.plan_extraction_plan: The number and percentage of participants with a Gleason score of 7 or less in the treatment arm (SOC + AAP) are not explicitly reported in the provided PDF. While the "Metastatic disease risk group" is reported in Table 1 and defined in the text (page 3, 4) using Gleason score ≥8 as one criterion, the breakdown of Gleason scores (e.g., ≤7 or ≥8) for each arm is not provided directly.

Gleason score - N (%) | \u2264 7 | Contr.plan_extraction_plan: The number and percentage of patients with a Gleason score of 7 or less in the control arm (SOC-alone) are not explicitly reported in the provided PDF. While the "Metastatic disease risk group" is reported in Table 1 and defined in the text (page 3, 4) using Gleason score ≥8 as one criterion, the breakdown of Gleason scores (e.g., ≤7 or ≥8) for each arm is not provided directly.

Gleason score - N (%) | \u2265 8 | Treat.plan_extraction_plan: The number and percentage of participants with a Gleason score of 8 or higher in the treatment arm (SOC + AAP) are not explicitly reported in the provided PDF. While the "Metastatic disease risk group" is reported in Table 1 (Chunk 0, page 5) and defined in the text (Chunk 5, page 3; Chunk 6, page 4) as "high-risk disease defined as at least two of: ≥3 bone metastases; visceral metastases; Gleason score ≥8," the "High-risk" category is a composite, and the specific N (%) for Gleason score ≥8 alone is not provided for the treatment arm.

Gleason score - N (%) | \u2265 8 | Contr.plan_extraction_plan: The number and percentage of patients with a Gleason score of 8 or higher in the control arm (SOC-alone) are not explicitly reported in the provided PDF. While the "Metastatic disease risk group" is reported in Table 1 (Chunk 0, page 5) and defined in the text (Chunk 5, page 3; Chunk 6, page 4) as "high-risk disease defined as at least two of: ≥3 bone metastases; visceral metastases; Gleason score ≥8," the "High-risk" category is a composite, and the specific N (%) for Gleason score ≥8 alone is not provided for the control arm.

NCT.plan_extraction_plan: The NCT identifier is reported in the title of the article on page 1: "STAMPEDE randomised trial (NCT00268476)". It is also mentioned on page 12 under "ETHICS STATEMENT": "The trial identification for STAMPEDE is NCT00268476 (clinicaltrials.gov)".

PubMed ID.plan_extraction_plan: PubMed ID is not reported in the PDF. The DOI is provided, but not the PubMed ID directly.

Trial Name.evidence: 5-year follow-up results from the STAMPEDE randomised trial (NCT00268476)
Trial Name.plan_extraction_plan: The trial name is "STAMPEDE randomised trial" as stated in the title on page 1: "5-year follow-up results from the STAMPEDE randomised trial (NCT00268476)".

Year.evidence: Received: 10 December 2021 Revised: 15 February 2022 Accepted: 22 February 2022
Year.plan_extraction_plan: The publication year is "2022" as stated at the top of page 1: "Received: 10 December 2021 Revised: 15 February 2022 Accepted: 22 February 2022 DOI: 10.1002/ijc.34018". It is also stated at the bottom of page 1: "2022 The Authors. International Journal of Cancer published by John Wiley & Sons Ltd on behalf of UICC."

Full Pub or Abstract.plan_extraction_plan: This is a full publication, indicated by the multiple pages of detailed methods, results, and discussion sections, as well as the journal name "International Journal of Cancer" and page numbering (422-434).

Phase.plan_extraction_plan: The phase is mentioned on page 3 in the "KEYWORDS" section: "phase III". It is also mentioned on page 13 in reference 15: "a randomised controlled phase 3 trial".

Original/Follow Up.plan_extraction_plan: The title on page 1 explicitly states "5-year follow-up results". The abstract on page 2 also mentions: "This long-term analysis in metastatic patients was planned for 3 years after the first results."

Median Age (years) | Treatment.plan_extraction_plan: The median age for the treatment arm (SOC + AAP) is reported in Table 1, "Baseline characteristics", on page 5. Locate the row "Age (years)" and the sub-row "Median (IQR)". The value in the column "SOC + AAP" is "67 (62-71)".

Median Age (years) | Control.plan_extraction_plan: The median age for the control arm (SOC-alone) is reported in Table 1, "Baseline characteristics", on page 5. Locate the row "Age (years)" and the sub-row "Median (IQR)". The value in the column "SOC-alone" is "67 (62-72)".

Median Follow-Up Duration (mo).evidence: The database was locked for this analysis on 3 April 2020. Median follow-up was 73 months (6.1 years).
Median Follow-Up Duration (mo).plan_extraction_plan: The median follow-up duration is reported in the "RESULTS" section. Extract the value "73" from the sentence: "The database was locked for this analysis on 3 April 2020. Median follow-up was 73 months (6.1 years)."

Median On-Treatment Duration (mo) | Cont.plan_extraction_plan: The text states that "All patients received lifelong ADT" (androgen deprivation therapy) in the control arm. While ADT is the treatment for the control arm, a specific median duration in months for this "lifelong" therapy is not reported in the document.

Median PFS (mo) | Overall | Treatment.plan_extraction_plan: The document reports Restricted Mean Survival Time (RMST) for progression-free survival, not median progression-free survival. For the SOC + AAP group, the RMST for progression-free survival is 62 months (95% CI: 59-66) as shown in Table 2 on page 6.

Median PFS (mo) | Overall | Control.plan_extraction_plan: The document reports Restricted Mean Survival Time (RMST) for progression-free survival, not median progression-free survival. For the SOC-alone group, the RMST for progression-free survival is 47 months (95% CI: 43-51) as shown in Table 2 on page 6.

It does not report median progression-free survival specifically for "High volume" patients defined by CHAARTED criteria. While the discussion mentions that analyses were carried out using CHAARTED criteria, the results are not presented in the provided tables.

No. of Deaths - N | Treatment.plan_extraction_plan: Extract the value from Table 2, under the "SOC + AAP" column, row "Overall survival" and sub-row "Events". The value is 244.

No. of Deaths - N | Control.plan_extraction_plan: Extract the value from Table 2, under the "SOC-alone" column, row "Overall survival" and sub-row "Events". The value is 329.

Number of Arms Included.plan_extraction_plan: Not reported. The paper describes a specific comparison within the STAMPEDE trial, which involved two arms (SOC-alone and SOC + AAP). However, the STAMPEDE trial is described as a "multiarm, multistage platform trial" (Chunk 5, page 2, Abstract) and the CONSORT diagram (Chunk 1, page 5, Figure 1) shows patients allocated to "other treatment groups" (N = 835), indicating more than two arms in the overall trial. The total count of all arms in the entire STAMPEDE trial is not explicitly stated as a numerical value.

ORR - N (%) | Treatment | Overall.plan_extraction_plan: Not reported. The study focuses on survival outcomes (overall survival, failure-free survival, progression-free survival, metastatic progression-free survival, skeletal-related events, and disease-specific survival) rather than objective response rates (complete response + partial response).

ORR - N (%) | Treatment | CR.plan_extraction_plan: Not reported. The study focuses on survival outcomes (overall survival, failure-free survival, progression-free survival, metastatic progression-free survival, skeletal-related events, and disease-specific survival) rather than objective response rates (complete response + partial response).

ORR - N (%) | Treatment | SD.plan_extraction_plan: Not reported. The study focuses on survival outcomes (overall survival, failure-free survival, progression-free survival, metastatic progression-free survival, skeletal-related events, and disease-specific survival) rather than objective response rates (complete response + partial response).

ORR - N (%) | Treatment | PD.plan_extraction_plan: Not reported as a percentage of patients with progressive disease in the context of an objective response rate assessment. The study reports "Events" for "Progression-free survival" and "Metastatic PFS" (Table 2, page 6; Table 3, page 8), which are counts of progression events over time, not a snapshot percentage of patients with PD.

ORR - N (%) | Control | Overall.plan_extraction_plan: Not reported. The study focuses on survival outcomes (overall survival, failure-free survival, progression-free survival, metastatic progression-free survival, skeletal-related events, and disease-specific survival) rather than objective response rates (complete response + partial response).

OS Rate (%) | High volume | Treatment.plan_extraction_plan: The document classifies patients into "High-risk" and "Low-risk" groups using LATITUDE criteria (page 4, section 2.3). The text on page 8 states that the LATITUDE and CHAARTED systems for defining metastatic disease risk group "largely coincided" and that "There was no evidence that the classifier used affected the overall conclusion". Therefore, the "High-risk" data from Table 3 is used. The value is reported in Table 3, under the "Overall survival" section, in the "High-risk" column, row "SOC + AAP" and column "% alive at 5 years, (95% CI)".

OS Rate (%) | High volume | Control.plan_extraction_plan: The document classifies patients into "High-risk" and "Low-risk" groups using LATITUDE criteria (page 4, section 2.3). The text on page 8 states that the LATITUDE and CHAARTED systems for defining metastatic disease risk group "largely coincided" and that "There was no evidence that the classifier used affected the overall conclusion". Therefore, the "High-risk" data from Table 3 is used. The value is reported in Table 3, under the "Overall survival" section, in the "High-risk" column, row "SOC-alone" and column "% alive at 5 years, (95% CI)".

Therefore, the "Low-risk" data from Table 3 is used. The value is reported in Table 3, under the "Overall survival" section, in the "Low-risk" column, row "SOC + AAP" and column "% alive at 5 years, (95% CI)".

OS Rate (%) | Low volume | Control.plan_extraction_plan: The document classifies patients into "High-risk" and "Low-risk" groups using LATITUDE criteria (page 4, section 2.3). The text on page 8 states that the LATITUDE and CHAARTED systems for defining metastatic disease risk group "largely coincided" and that "There was no evidence that the classifier used affected the overall conclusion". Therefore, the "Low-risk" data from Table 3 is used. The value is reported in Table 3, under the "Overall survival" section, in the "Low-risk" column, row "SOC-alone" and column "% alive at 5 years, (95% CI)".

PFS Rate (%) | Overall | Treatment.evidence:
Progression-free survival
% event-free at 5 years (95% CI) 54% (50-59)
PFS Rate (%) | Overall | Treatment.plan_extraction_plan: The progression-free survival rate for the treatment arm (SOC + AAP) is reported in Table 2, under the "Progression-free survival" section, as "% event-free at 5 years (95% CI)". The value is 54% (50-59). The hazard ratio for SOC + AAP versus SOC-alone is 0.58 (95% CI: 0.49-0.69), P < .0001.

Primary Endpoint(s).plan_extraction_plan: The primary outcome measure is "overall survival". This is explicitly stated in the Abstract on page 3: "The primary outcome measure was overall survival." and reiterated in Section 2.4 "Outcome measures and statistical analysis" on page 4: "The primary outcome for this comparison was overall survival...".

Quality of Life Scale.plan_extraction_plan: The specific Quality of Life Scale used or its results are not reported in this PDF for the abiraterone comparison. The abbreviations list "QOL, quality of life", and the discussion section mentions "patient-related QoL outcomes previously supported the use of abiraterone over docetaxel" (page 10, text, referring to reference 14). However, this paper does not present the QL scale or its findings. The "Outcome measures and statistical analysis" section (page 4, text) does not list Quality of Life as a primary or secondary outcome for this analysis.

Quality of Life reported.plan_extraction_plan: The "Abbreviations" section on page 1 defines QOL as "quality of life". The "Outcome measures and statistical analysis" section on page 4 lists the primary and secondary outcomes for this comparison, and Quality of Life is not among them. The "Discussion" section on page 10 mentions "patient-related QoL outcomes previously supported the use of abiraterone over docetaxel," referencing a separate publication (Rush et al., 2021, reference 14 on page 13). This indicates that QoL was assessed in the broader STAMPEDE trial but is not reported as a result in this specific paper. Therefore, Quality of Life results are not reported in this PDF.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | S.plan_extraction_plan: The study reports on "newly diagnosed" patients, which is synonymous with synchronous metastases. This is stated in the text in Chunk 5 on page 3: "94% newly-diagnosed" and in Chunk 6 on page 4: "For this comparison in STAMPEDE, eligible patients had metastatic prostate cancer that was newly-diagnosed or relapsing after previous local therapy". Also, in Chunk 7 on page 10: "Most patients in STAMPEDE had de novo metastatic disease".

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | M.plan_extraction_plan: The study reports on "relapsing" patients, which indicates metachronous disease. This is stated in Table 1 (Chunk 0) on page 5 under "Eligibility category": "Relapsing" (5% in SOC-alone, 7% in SOC + AAP, 6% overall).

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | H.evidence: Table 3: Primary and Secondary outcomes, by metastatic disease risk group using LATITUDE criteria
Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | H.plan_extraction_plan: The study reports results by "high-risk" metastatic disease group, which is defined using criteria (≥3 bone metastases; visceral metastases; Gleason score ≥8) that indicate high volume/burden. This is found in Table 3 (Chunk 3) on page 8, which presents "Primary and Secondary outcomes, by metastatic disease risk group using LATITUDE criteria" for the "High-risk" group. The definition is in Chunk 6 on page 4: "high-risk disease defined as at least two of: ≥3 bone metastases; visceral metastases; Gleason score ≥8."

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | L.evidence: Table 3: Primary and Secondary outcomes, by metastatic disease risk group using LATITUDE criteria
Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | L.plan_extraction_plan: The study reports results by "low-risk" metastatic disease group, which is defined using criteria that indicate low volume/burden. This is found in Table 3 (Chunk 3) on page 8, which presents "Primary and Secondary outcomes, by metastatic disease risk group using LATITUDE criteria" for the "Low-risk" group. The definition is in Chunk 6 on page 4: "high-risk disease defined as at least two of: ≥3 bone metastases; visceral metastases; Gleason score ≥8." (implying low-risk is less than these criteria).

Treatment Arm 1 Regimen.value: Androgen deprivation therapy (ADT) + abiraterone acetate 1000 mg daily + prednisolone 5 mg daily
Treatment Arm 1 Regimen.evidence: All patients received lifelong ADT using gonadotrophin-releasing hormone (GnRH) agonists, antagonists, or orchidectomy. Patients allocated to the SOC + AAP group were also planned to receive abiraterone (1000 mg daily) with prednisolone (5 mg daily).
Treatment Arm 1 Regimen.plan_extraction_plan: The treatment regimen for the experimental arm (SOC + AAP) is reported in the abstract and the methods section. The abstract states "SOC + AAP" is "standard-of-care plus abiraterone acetate + prednisone/prednisolone" and "Standard-of-care (SOC) was androgen deprivation therapy." The methods section further specifies the dosages. Extract "Androgen deprivation therapy (ADT) + abiraterone acetate 1000 mg daily + prednisolone 5 mg daily".

Treatment Arm(s).evidence: Abiraterone acetate plus prednisolone (AAP) previously demonstrated improved survival in STAMPEDE, a multiarm, multistage platform trial in men starting long-term hormone therapy for prostate cancer.
Treatment Arm(s).plan_extraction_plan: The name of the experimental arm is "SOC + AAP". This is explicitly stated in the abstract and consistently used throughout the document to refer to the abiraterone acetate plus prednisolone arm.

Treatment Class.value: Standard-of-care (SOC), Standard-of-care plus abiraterone acetate and prednisolone (SOC + AAP)
Treatment Class.plan_extraction_plan: The treatment classes are "Standard-of-care (SOC)" and "Standard-of-care plus abiraterone acetate and prednisolone (SOC + AAP)". This information is explicitly stated in the abstract and the "Procedures" section.

Trial.plan_extraction_plan: The trial name is reported in the title of the article. Extract "STAMPEDE randomised trial (NCT00268476)" from the text on page 1.

Type of Therapy.value: Standard-of-care (SOC) was androgen deprivation therapy (ADT) using gonadotrophin-releasing hormone (GnRH) agonists, antagonists, or orchidectomy. The experimental arm (SOC + AAP) received SOC plus abiraterone acetate (1000 mg daily) and prednisolone (5 mg daily)
Type of Therapy.evidence: Abiraterone acetate plus prednisolone for metastatic patients starting hormone therapy
Type of Therapy.plan_extraction_plan: The type of therapy is described in the abstract and the methods section. From the abstract on page 1: "Abiraterone acetate plus prednisolone for metastatic patients starting hormone therapy". From the abstract on page 2: "Standard-of-care (SOC) was androgen deprivation therapy." and "Abiraterone acetate plus prednisolone (AAP) previously demonstrated improved survival in STAMPEDE, a multiarm, multistage platform trial in men starting long-term hormone therapy for prostate cancer." From Section 2.3 "Procedures" on page 4: "All patients received lifelong ADT using gonadotrophin-releasing hormone (GnRH) agonists, antagonists, or orchidectomy. Patients allocated to the SOC + AAP group were also planned to receive abiraterone (1000 mg daily) with prednisolone (5 mg daily)." Value: Standard-of-care (SOC) was androgen deprivation therapy (ADT) using gonadotrophin-releasing hormone (GnRH) agonists, antagonists, or orchidectomy. The experimental arm (SOC + AAP) received SOC plus abiraterone acetate (1000 mg daily) and prednisolone (5 mg daily).
